name: Set Alert
summary: Sets a price alert for a trading symbol. Make a POST call with a jData JSON object describing the symbol, alert type, comparison value and validity; returns the created alert id on success.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid*
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

json_fields.name: d
json_fields.description: Data to be compared with LTP

json_fields.name: remarks*
json_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input 2) Session Expired

notes: The full request is sent as a form-urlencoded query-style string with two parts joined by '&': jData={...json...} and jKey=<session key>. The success response is a JSON ARRAY containing a single object (unlike the failure response, which is a bare object). The curl shows 'https://BaseURL/SetAlert' as a placeholder; the real base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI. Field 'd' (data to be compared with LTP) is the only non-mandatory jData field; all others (uid, tsym, exch, ai_t, validity, remarks) are mandatory per the trailing '*'. validity possible values are 'DAY or GTT'. emsg appears only on error and signals Invalid Input or Session Expired. The success response uses curly/smart quotes around al_id verbatim in the source PDF.
